principleEntry.title: Use the scale
principleEntry.description: Every spacing value comes from the spacing scale. Reach for the nearest scale step rather than a custom value. When two adjacent steps both seem wrong, the layout — not the scale — usually needs rethinking.

principleEntry.title: Density over decoration
principleEntry.description: Spacing exists to group and separate content, not to fill the canvas. Prefer the smallest spacing that still makes the grouping clear, so more content stays within reach without scrolling.

principleEntry.title: Consistent rhythm
principleEntry.description: Related elements share the same spacing so the eye can predict where the next item begins. Vary spacing only to signal a change in relationship, never for visual variety alone.

items.title: Functional first
items.description: Color carries meaning before it carries style. Choose a color for the role it plays — danger, success, emphasis — and let the palette follow from those roles rather than from brand decoration.

items.title: Accessible by default
items.description: Every foreground and background pairing in the system meets WCAG 2.2 AA contrast. A color combination that fails contrast is not offered as an option, so teams cannot reach for an inaccessible pairing by accident.

items.title: Semantic, not literal
items.description: Reference colors by their role ('color-text-danger'), never by their value ('red-600'). This lets the system retheme — including dark mode — without touching product code.
